---
title: "Tools"
---

Tools I built with the help of LLMs. Each one started the same way: I hit a gap
in how I work or live, and decided to close it myself. They are grouped by the need they answer,
my research and data work on one side, everyday life on the other.

[Research & data]{.eyebrow}

::::: {.card-grid}

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" src="tools/viset/venn-drug-targets.png" alt="A VISET proportional Venn diagram of drug-target genes for Fluoxetine, Ibuprofen and Acetaminophen, with every gene name printed inside its region">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [VISET](tools/viset/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[Python library · open source]{.paper-meta}

Area-proportional Venn/Euler and UpSet diagrams that print the actual member names inside each
region, not just the counts. Built in Python for comparing gene sets at a glance.
:::

::::

:::::

[Lifestyle]{.eyebrow}

::::: {.card-grid}

:::: {.pcard}
<img class="pcard-media" style="object-fit:cover;object-position:center" src="tools/focus-pocus/app-interface.png" alt="The Focus Pocus timer window with a running timer and controls">

::: {.pcard-body}
### [Focus Pocus](tools/focus-pocus/index.qmd){.stretched-link}
[Desktop app · in development]{.paper-meta}

A focus timer that notices when you drift off, whether it's an idle mouse or a YouTube tab, and
beeps you back louder and louder until you return. Built to get my PhD thesis written.
:::

::::

:::::
